objective: Deliver the first runnable safety vertical slice: a versioned capability manifest, deterministic allow/ask/deny decisions, bounded rate and session budgets, revocation and feature flags, operator emergency controls, hash-chained audit records, and an owner-facing Dev Console control surface.
non_goals: Do not integrate a real LLM, TTS engine, memory store, game action, stream platform or model-generated code execution.
Do not claim full content moderation, PII detection, prompt-injection classification or M02 release readiness in this slice.
Do not expose the safety service outside loopback or add remote authentication in this local-owner slice.
Do not run repeat, soak, chaos, 20-run or full release verification.
contracts: Unknown, expired, revoked, critical and feature-disabled capabilities deny deterministically.
Only an owner-trust local control request can reset emergency stop, unmute output, enable a feature or remove revocation.
Emergency stop is applied before audit I/O and remains restrictive even if audit writing fails.
Every normal policy decision and operator control appends a bounded-field audit record chained by SHA-256; no caller-supplied secret or raw prompt is accepted into the audit API.
Rate limits use monotonic time, session budgets are bounded, and evaluation can be non-consuming while authorization consumes only an allow decision.
The browser Console calls the real loopback safety API and never simulates policy results client-side.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library, browser platform APIs and existing Hina runtime modules.
acceptance_criteria: The owner can inspect the loaded manifest, safety state and recent verified audit records in Hina Dev Console.
The owner can authorize a known capability and observe allow, ask or deny with a stable reason code.
The owner can toggle emergency stop, mute, feature flags and capability revocation, then immediately observe policy enforcement.
Unknown, expired, revoked, rate-limited, budget-exhausted and critical code-execution capability cases are covered by deterministic unit tests.
Audit tampering is detected and emergency stop remains active when audit append is unavailable.
Focused safety and core runtime unit tests plus a real startup smoke pass once.
